zh.lifeSummary: 當LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT於1509年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿申維爾索普時，她的父親Sir Edmund II Knyvett23歲，母親Jane Bourchier19歲。她於大約1526年與Richard Sayer在英國艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1595年11月12日在荷蘭努-布拉班特享年86歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT于1509年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可阿申維爾索普时，她的父亲Sir Edmund II Knyvett23岁，母亲Jane Bourchier19岁。她于大约1526年与Richard Sayer在英国艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1595年11月12日在荷兰努-布拉班特享年86岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

nameMeanings.name: KNYVETT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Knivett  .

variants.name: Knivett
meanings: English: from a Norman French pronunciation of Knight , resulting from the Norman French difficulty with the h of Old English cniht.

nameMeanings.name: LADY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: ANNE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: BOURCHIER
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin an occupational name for either a scribe or binder of books (a derivative of Middle English boke ‘book’, Old English bōc), or else a bleacher of cloth (a derivative of Middle English bouken ‘to steep in lye’, Middle Dutch būken).

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: From Old Norse: apparently an agent derivative of biarga ‘to help’. Earlier forms of the name are Birghir and Byrghir. It has been in use from the Viking period to the present day, and it may in some cases have been chosen as a masculine form of Birgit .

historicEvents.description: The Catholic King Charles I of Spain ruled the Netherlands.
William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
The siege of Leiden between the rebels and the Spanish. 1574 The dikes were opened allowing a Dutch fleet to sail over the flood waters and rescue Leiden.

The Union of Utrecht brought together seven northern, Protestant provinces of the Netherlands against the Catholics. Known as the United Provinces, they become the foundation of the Dutch Republic. The Treaty of Utrecht was signed, marking the beginning of the Dutch Republic.
After the signing of the Treaty of Utrecht in 1579, Brabant became a regular battlefield between the Republic of the Netherlands (Protestant) and Spain (Catholic).

en.lifeSummary: When LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT was born in 1509, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Edmund II Knyvett, was 23 and her mother, Jane Bourchier, was 19. She married Richard Sayer about 1526, in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 12 November 1595, in North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 86, and was buried in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT est née en 1509, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Edmund II Knyvett, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Jane Bourchier, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Sayer environ 1526, à Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 novembre 1595, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 86 ans, et a été enterrée à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT 1509 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Edmund II Knyvett 23 und ihre Mutter Jane Bourchier 19. Sie hat um 1526 in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England Richard Sayer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 12. November 1595 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT è nata il nel 1509 nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Edmund II Knyvett, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Jane Bourchier, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Richard Sayer circa 1526, nel Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 12 novembre 1595, nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 86 anni, e fu sepolta a Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETTが1509年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペで生まれた時，父Sir Edmund II Knyvettは23歳，母Jane Bourchierは19歳であった。約1526年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスターコルチェスター カストルでRichard Sayerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1595年11月12日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで86歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT(이)가 1509년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 애슈웰소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edmund II Knyvett(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Bourchier(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1526년 영국 에세스 코체스터 코체스터 카스틀에서 Richard Sayer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1595년 11월 12일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 86세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT nasceu em 1509, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Edmund II Knyvett, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Jane Bourchier, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Sayer aproximadamente 1526, em Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 12 de novembro de 1595, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 86 anos, e foi sepultada em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT родилась в 1509 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Edmund II Knyvett было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Jane Bourchier, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Sayer около 1526 г. в местности колчестир кастл, колчестир, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 ноября 1595 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 86 лет и была похоронена в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT nació en 1509, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Edmund II Knyvett, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Jane Bourchier, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Richard Sayer aproximadamente 1526, en Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 12 de noviembre de 1595, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 86 años, y fue sepultada en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
